steps.title: Spot the expanding scope
steps.content: The passage moves from exact replicas to towers that also honor the design — a natural expansion of the theme, not a contradiction.

steps.title: Apply the additive relationship
steps.content:
The logical relationship is addition: the first sentence notes exact replicas exist, and the second adds that non-replica towers also pay homage to the design. The blank needs a transition word signaling that a further related point is being added.
  • Choice A — On the contrary,: signals direct opposition, so it fails because non-replica towers honoring the design does not contradict the existence of exact replicas. ✘
  • Choice B — Consequently,: signals a result caused by the first sentence, so it fails because the existence of homage towers is not caused by Tbilisi's replica. ✘
  • Choice C — Invariably,: signals that something always happens without exception, so it fails because the sentence is making a specific claim about certain towers, not a universal rule. ✘
  • Choice D — Moreover,: signals an additional point that extends the scope of the first, so it fits — beyond exact replicas, towers that merely honor the design also exist. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
